symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe escalation of localised wound pain over 12 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sharp rise in pain in a previously quiescent chronic ulcer is a cardinal sign of new wound infection.
Severe pain must be checked against 'pain out of proportion' — here it tracks the visible infection, arguing against necrotising fasciitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy purulent malodorous green exudate (change from minimal serous fluid)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New purulence and malodour indicate a high bacterial load and established local wound infection.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading cellulitis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Surrounding erythema and warmth define peri-wound cellulitis; extent and induration need in-person palpation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low-grade temperature signals early systemic response; caregiver-reported so admissible remotely but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Slough and necrotic tissue in the wound bed with absent healthy granulation (5 cm x 4 cm ulcer, no gross undermining)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Devitalised tissue in the wound bed is a nidus for infection; requires in-person inspection and debridement — sealed clinician finding.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading cellulitis of the lower limb
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Induration extent is a marker of soft-tissue involvement, measurable only on palpation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia is a soft early systemic marker; exact rate is a sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Maintained blood pressure and normal mentation (no rigors, not confused)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved haemodynamics and cognition make frank sepsis unlikely and keep the baseline at same-day in-person rather than emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crepitus, no haemorrhagic bullae, no pain out of proportion, no dusky skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the hard necrotising-fasciitis markers argues strongly against the surgical emergency mimic.

symptoms.symptom_name: No generalised unilateral calf swelling, negative Homan's

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain and erythema localised circumferentially to the open wound rather than diffuse calf swelling lower the probability of DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic venous insufficiency with haemosiderin staining

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The underlying venous disease is the substrate for the non-healing ulcer that became infected.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep wound swab microscopy, culture and sensitivities

investigation_details.expected_result: Heavy bacterial load; organism identification pending (commonly Staphylococcus aureus in this setting) — SEALED expected result, obtainable only in person.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Empirical antibiotics are not culture-directed and resistant or atypical organisms may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Culture confirms the infection and directs antibiotic choice; cannot be obtained via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute wound infection triad on a chronic ulcer
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute infection of chronic venous leg ulcer
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The co-occurrence of new severe pain, purulent malodorous exudate and peri-wound erythema in a previously stable ulcer is the recognisable signature of acute local wound infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Deep wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivities (in person)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the causative organism and directs antibiotic therapy; must accompany same-day source control.
Characterises the wound bed and removes devitalised tissue; cannot be done remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinician vital-sign set (BP, HR, RR, SpO2, temperature)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the early systemic signature and screens for evolving sepsis at the in-person visit.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Lower-limb venous/arterial assessment (e.g. ABPI) once infection settles
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides ongoing compression and long-term ulcer management; not part of the acute infective work-up.
